description:
**Milestone:** HyPE Pre-Computed Query Embeddings

Add hype_queries TEXT column (JSON array) and hype_embeddings BLOB column to chunks table. Add a HyPE background worker in KnowledgeStoreService: after embeddings are computed for a chunk, if hype_queries is null, call Haiku with prompt: 'Generate 3 short, specific questions that this text answers. Return only a JSON array of strings, no explanation. Text: {chunk.heading} {chunk.content.slice(0,500)}'. Parse the JSON array, store in hype_queries. Then embed each question (via EmbeddingService.embedBatch), average the resulting vectors into a single representative query embedding, store in hype_embeddings BLOB. This runs as a background async job after the embedding worker completes, non-blocking. Add getHypeStatus(projectPath) returning {total, hype_ready, pending}. Configuration: hypeEnabled: boolean in KnowledgeStoreSettings (default: true when hybridRetrieval enabled). Rate-limit the Haiku calls to max 10/minute to avoid API quota issues.

**Files to Modify:**
- apps/server/src/services/knowledge-store-service.ts
- libs/types/src/knowledge.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] HyPE background worker generates 3 queries per chunk via Haiku
- [ ] hype_queries stored as JSON array, hype_embeddings as averaged Float32Array BLOB
- [ ] Worker rate-limited to 10 Haiku calls/minute
- [ ] getHypeStatus() returns progress statistics
- [ ] hypeEnabled setting toggles the worker
- [ ] npm run build:server passes

**Complexity:** medium
title: HyPE Ingestion — Hypothetical Query Generation
